what: THE ASK - every question the live probe puts to the client, with the method for reading each answer. Verdicts are null until the probe runs.

verdict_matrix.X=true, noX=true: IGNORED         both true => no-op / not evaluated

two_mechanisms.why_this_matters: `@unit` and `[flag]` are NOT the same mechanism and do NOT share a proof method. Conflating them is the easiest way to write a confident wrong guide.

@unit / target=.mechanism: HYPOTHESIS: a pass-through STRING. SecureCmdOptionParse appears to return whatever follows @; support lives DOWNSTREAM in the handler's own special-casing (/cast checks target=='cursor' -> Custom_HandleTerrainClick) and in unit resolution.
@unit / target=.independent_support: wowpedia/Macro_conditionals states '@unitId : Replace with any valid unitId' and '`target=` is also an alias for `@`' - a RETAIL doc describing the same pass-through shape the CoA handler code implies. Two directions, one hypothesis. Still a hypothesis.
@unit / target=.consequence: the polarity matrix does NOT apply; there is likely no [no@cursor].
@unit / target=.probe_row_that_settles_it: SecureCmdOptionParse("[@banana] X") - if a nonsense unit returns target="banana", @ is pass-through and the vocabulary question does not apply to it at all.
@unit / target=.status: HYPOTHESIS - not asserted. One probe row settles it.

[flag].mechanism: actually EVALUATED by the C parser (combat, mod:shift, group, ...).
[flag].consequence: THIS is the vocabulary question, and what the polarity matrix is for.

two_mechanisms.name_collision_worth_knowing: [cursor] (a FLAG - 'what the cursor is holding', per wowwiki-archive) is NOT @cursor (a TARGET - Ascension's terrain-click). Same word, two mechanisms.

control_row.why: decides how to read ALL 13 target rows. If a nonsense unit returns target='banana', @ is a PASS-THROUGH string and the vocabulary question does not apply to targets at all. Run and read this FIRST.

standing_limits.asked_only: A probe proves only what it ASKS. This ask-list came from the wiki chain, so the rim stays OPEN - the output is a proven set with an honest rim, NEVER an enumeration.
standing_limits.true_of_the_moment: A verdict is true of the CONTEXT it ran in. PROVEN-FALSE means 'false right now', not 'unsupported' - the pair separates those. But context-bound flags (flying, mounted, indoors, combat, stance:N) want a SECOND run in a different context to be meaningful.
standing_limits.absence_proves_nothing: A conditional no wiki lists is UN-ASKED, not unsupported. Demonstrated mechanically: ['@pettarget'] witnessed in this client's own Lua and in NO wiki.
